gates.purpose: Authorize the project to proceed from idea to defined scope.
exit_criteria: Problem statement and success criteria are approved.
Decision owner and stakeholder map are explicit.
Funding, capacity, or sponsorship assumptions are documented.

gates.purpose: Freeze the accountable requirements baseline before design completion.
exit_criteria: Requirements have identifiers and acceptance criteria.
Traceability links requirements to future design and validation.
Information assets and service objectives are agreed.

gates.purpose: Approve the target architecture and operating design before build.
exit_criteria: Design covers functional, operational, security, and data concerns.
Control obligations are mapped to design and procedure references.
Open design risks have owners and disposition.

gates.purpose: Ensure execution is controlled and changes are governable.
exit_criteria: Work breakdown, ownership, and reporting cadence are active.
Active changes are traceable through mission and change ledgers.
Known risks, assumptions, issues, and dependencies are current.

gates.purpose: Demonstrate that the release candidate is acceptable for release decision.
exit_criteria: Planned validation is complete or approved with explicit exceptions.
Requirements are traced to test evidence and defects.
Security findings and residual risks are dispositioned.

gates.purpose: Decide whether release and cutover may proceed.
exit_criteria: Operational, deployment, and rollback readiness are approved.
Post-deployment verification criteria are explicit.
Support and user-facing handoff materials are available.

gates.purpose: Formally close delivery and capture reusable learning.
exit_criteria: Open critical items have a follow-up owner.
Lessons learned are recorded for reuse.
Closure approval is recorded.
